meaning: adj.潮湿的,多阴雨的,溅湿的,溅污的,肥大的
examples: He has little patience for sloppy work from colleagues.
When your clothes look sloppy, so do you.
Embroidery calls for great care. There must not be even a single sloppy stitch.
Coach Andy Robinson echoed Parks's comments, and said they never recovered from a sloppy start.
Djokovic's serve, sloppy as recently as last season, is now precise, fluid and, at times, devastating.
